ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Feb-17-2023 Set For Order To Show Cause On Aug-03-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Aug-26-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Thomas Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant Chevron USA.
Robert J. Bugatto of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler, LLC.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). The Court did not consider the conditional non-opposition filed by Morse Tec, LLC for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.)

The trial date of January 17, 2023 is vacated. The new trial date is October 17, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is October 28, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 13, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 30, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 14, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 29, 2022. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-30-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than July 14, 2022, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than July 14, 2022, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for September 1, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 2, 2022. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 16, 2022. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by October 7, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, October 13, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
